source: T06 dry-run model routing hook integration completion
T07 install/load and dist strategy evidence
user-approved redacted global OpenCode config inspection for GitHub plugin source syntax
user follow-up decision for default model-selection toast notification

purpose: Reusable implementation context for future agents/calls.
Cache reviewer-approved plan constraints for OpenCode Gem Team plugin implementation.
Replace invalid old four-wave plan with executable PoC-gated twelve-wave DAG.
applies_to: OpenCode Gem Team plugin implementation planning
agent sync and model tier routing
non_goals: This planning update does not implement plugin code.
No concrete provider model IDs, provider model names, API keys, tokens, or secrets.

reviewer_critic_blocking_findings_fixed.finding: Old plan had four waves and same-wave dependency/conflict problems.
reviewer_critic_blocking_findings_fixed.fix_summary: Replaced with 12 waves, one task per wave, so no dependency edge or conflicts_with entry can land in the same wave.

reviewer_critic_blocking_findings_fixed.finding: Dependency edges lacked explicit top-level contracts.
reviewer_critic_blocking_findings_fixed.fix_summary: Added C01-C21 top-level contracts matching every dependency edge.

reviewer_critic_blocking_findings_fixed.finding: Feasibility was too late and release/docs/dist paths overclaimed unverified GitHub install behavior.
reviewer_critic_blocking_findings_fixed.fix_summary: Moved feasibility PoC to T01 wave 1 and made hooks/docs/dist/release path PoC-dependent.

reviewer_critic_blocking_findings_fixed.finding: dist was assumed committed by default.
reviewer_critic_blocking_findings_fixed.fix_summary: dist is optional and only produced if T01 proves necessary; validate:dist freshness required if present.

reviewer_critic_blocking_findings_fixed.finding: Sync metadata and body/orchestration preservation were under-specified.
reviewer_critic_blocking_findings_fixed.fix_summary: Required upstream source URL, upstream commit, body/content hash, local slug, sync timestamp or batch id; body-preserve and orchestration sentinel guards added.
Added read-only GitHub guard, no-token/no-secret/no-telemetry restrictions, and dry-run/mock/local PoC-only provider behavior.

reviewer_critic_blocking_findings_fixed.finding: Docs could publish final commands before verification.
reviewer_critic_blocking_findings_fixed.fix_summary: README/examples must use Verified/Pending/Blocked statuses and cannot include final unverified install commands before PoC completion.

reviewer_critic_blocking_findings_fixed.finding: Security review scope was too narrow.
reviewer_critic_blocking_findings_fixed.fix_summary: T11 covers package.json, src/**, scripts/**, agents/** or generated agents, dist/** if present, test/**, README.md, examples/**, docs/installation-poc.md, docs/PRD.md, and this plan directory.

agent_registry: Exactly 16 canonical gem-* slugs must be present.
gem-orchestrator must not include itself as child/subagent.
model_routing: Priority chain: OpenCode agent.model > complexity_models[tier] > selected model.
Only simple, medium, and complex tiers are supported.
No per-agent, role, provider-router, auto-fallback, standalone provider model, or hardcoded model config.
sync_metadata: Every synced entry records upstream source URL.
Every synced entry records upstream commit.
Every synced entry records body/content hash.
Every synced entry records local slug.
Every synced entry records sync timestamp or sync batch id.
sync_network_guard: Do not read or output env secrets.
No POST, PUT, upload, remote mutation, or telemetry.
poc_guard: Default PoC does not call real providers.
Do not record API keys or tokens.
Do not leak concrete supplier model identifiers.
body_preserve_guard: Upstream agent body is preserved verbatim by default.
Non-metadata/header body diff fails unless allowlisted with reason and evidence.
orchestration_preserve_guard: phases must not be converted away or semantically rewritten.
DAG/waves must not be converted away or semantically rewritten.
task schemas must not be converted away or semantically rewritten.
failure handling must not be converted away or semantically rewritten.
memory/context envelope must not be converted away or semantically rewritten.
docs_guard: README/examples install instructions use Verified/Pending/Blocked three-state status.
No final unverified install commands before PoC completion.
GitHub install, dist, and package-manager conclusions remain PoC-dependent.

must_verify_in_T01: GitHub source syntax status; format observed from redacted global config, end-to-end install still Pending.
do_not_assume: Do not assume observed GitHub source syntax proves this repo installs or loads from GitHub.
Do not assume dist must be committed.
Do not assume package-manager behavior during GitHub load.
Do not assume actual model mutation can satisfy AC-20 without evidence.

open_questions.question: Does the observed GitHub source syntax load this repository as an OpenCode plugin end-to-end?
Is committed dist required for GitHub install/load?
Can model-routing hook mutation be proven locally without real provider calls?

why_relevant: Defines GitHub target, sync metadata, slug list, model routing, ACs, risks.
Authoritative implementation sequence after reviewer/critic fixes.
Blocks docs, hooks, dist, and release conclusions.

gotchas.text: Do not hardcode concrete model IDs or supplier model names even in tests, docs, examples, validation messages, or plan outputs.
Do not print env variables during sync/PoC because tokens/secrets may be present.
dist remains optional and PoC-dependent; producing dist without proof violates the plan.
If later conversion-layer work introduces body diffs, add parity checks before config injection and keep upstream bodies preserved unless an explicit allowlist has reason and evidence.

mvp_gates.name: agent load
mvp_gates.acceptance: Local/mock OpenCode load evidence shows 16 injected agents.

mvp_gates.name: agent.model preservation
mvp_gates.acceptance: Existing OpenCode agent.model remains highest priority and is never overwritten.

mvp_gates.name: tier routing
mvp_gates.acceptance: Only simple/medium/complex tier routing exists and no forbidden model dimensions are introduced.

facts.statement: Baseline commit is ee708a8f9046b46054776345f05bf62b37b188d7.
Plan now has 12 waves, 12 tasks, and 21 dependency contracts.
T01 is the early feasibility PoC gate and blocks hooks/docs/dist/release path conclusions.
GitHub install, dist, and package-manager conclusions are PoC-dependent and not confirmed.
T01 verified @opencode-ai/plugin@1.15.13 exposes PluginModule with optional id and required server Plugin.
T01 verified config, chat.message, and chat.params hooks exist in the installed SDK type surface.

T01 did not verify a documented GitHub plugin source syntax in official OpenCode docs; later user-approved redacted global config inspection observed syntax github:<owner>/<repo>#<ref>, while this repo's end-to-end GitHub install/load remains Pending.
T01 found chat.params output exposes parameters/options but not a direct model field, so direct model mutation remains Pending/risky.
T02 created a root TypeScript ESM scaffold with PluginModule default export id opencode-gem-team and a minimal config hook placeholder.
T02 added package scripts for build, typecheck, test, lint, sync:agents, validate:sync, and validate:no-model-ids.
T02 used built-in node:test after TypeScript build to keep the scaffold minimal and avoid adding a test framework dependency.
T02 kept dist ignored and did not add validate:dist because T01 still marks dist necessity as pending.

T03 implemented pure routing/types only; no provider/API calls, provider router, fallback chain, virtual models, profiles, telemetry, slash commands, global OpenCode config writes, or chat hook model mutation were added.
T03 resolveModel proves priority chain OpenCode agent.model > complexity_models[tier] > current selected model and preserves the input agent config object.
T03 supports only simple, medium, and complex tiers; missing or unknown tiers deterministically fallback to medium with reason metadata.
T03 role slugs are tier hints only; reasoning-critical roles can raise minimum tier but cannot map to models or introduce role_models/per-agent model dimensions.

T03 enhanced validate:no-model-ids to scan docs/plan and common model-assignment provider-style hardcoding patterns while avoiding false positives for scoped package names such as @opencode-ai/plugin.
T04 replaced sync/validate placeholders with read-only unauthenticated GitHub sync and validation scripts writing agents/generated/*.agent.md plus agents/generated/manifest.json.

T04 sync batch resolved upstream commit 0c2afe46dc0136439e668b04e551c465d68eaf39 and records source URL, raw URL, upstream commit, SHA-256 source body hash, local slug, body path, body size, sync timestamp, and sync batch id per agent.
T04 preserves upstream agent bodies verbatim in agents/generated and derives only manifest metadata for OpenCode compatibility and later config injection.

T04 validation verifies exact 16 canonical slugs, required sync metadata, local body hash parity, non-empty bodies, forbidden config/model/secret patterns, and gem-orchestrator self-route exclusion in routingTargets metadata.
T04 reviewer passed the upstream sync with no blocking findings and allowed continuation to Wave 5.
docs/PRD.yaml is absent by design in this repository; docs/PRD.md remains the PRD source of truth.
T05 added runtime generated-agent loading from agents/generated/manifest.json and agents/generated/*.agent.md with SHA-256 prompt parity checks.
T05 config injection initializes config.agent, injects exactly 16 Gem Team agents, marks gem-orchestrator primary, marks the other generated agents subagent, and never creates default model fields.
T05 non-destructive merge preserves existing OpenCode agent fields including model, prompt, mode, permission, and arbitrary user fields.
T05 injects no unsupported metadata into OpenCode agent config; manifest metadata remains audit-only.
T05 getGemOrchestratorRoutingTargets exposes manifest routingTargets and excludes gem-orchestrator.
T05 reviewer passed config hook injection with no blocking findings and allowed continuation to Wave 6.
T06 added src/agents/generated.ts constants exposing exactly the canonical generated Gem Team slug set and count.
T06 added previewModelRouting as a local dry-run helper that derives tier from structured signals and resolves the T03 priority chain with opaque model strings.
T06 wired a chat.params hook PoC that mutates only output.options.model in mock invocation when the resolved source is complexity_model; native agent.model remains preserved without hook override.
T06 AC-20 status is partial because mock/type evidence exists for chat.params output.options mutation, but actual OpenCode runtime request model mutation is not verified without provider/API calls.
T06 reviewer passed the dry-run routing/chat.params hook PoC with no blocking findings and allowed continuation to Wave 7.
T07 verified local package-path OpenCode plugin install in a temp fixture after adding package main and exports[./server] targeting dist/src/index.js.
T07 verified OpenCode agent list exposed 16 gem-* agents from the local plugin fixture, including primary gem-orchestrator.

T07 kept GitHub source install syntax Pending because official docs and CLI help did not verify a final GitHub plugin source syntax; later redacted global config evidence observed format github:<owner>/<repo>#<ref> only.
T07 added validate:dist and prepared dist/src inclusion because package-style OpenCode target detection and imports require built dist entrypoints when package exports target dist.
T07 observed OpenCode CLI version 1.15.13 during local install/load verification.
T07 reviewer passed install/dist/docs PoC with no blocking findings and allowed continuation to the final waves.
Final implementation wave hardened validate:dist with deterministic dist/src forbidden feature string scanning and defensive-validation allowlists.

Final hardening added test/validate-dist.test.ts coverage for forbidden strings failing, scoped defensive validation strings passing, and non-validation modules being unable to use forbidden config names.

Final verification passed sync:agents, validate:sync, npm test, typecheck, build, validate:no-model-ids, lint, and validate:dist; npm test reported 37 passing tests and validate:dist checked 30 dist/src files.
Final JSON parse checks passed for package.json, agents/generated/manifest.json, and context_envelope.json; plan.yaml smoke parse passed.
No provider/API calls and no global OpenCode config writes were introduced during final hardening.

Follow-up user-approved global OpenCode config inspection was read-only and redacted; only plugin array evidence with github:<owner>/<repo>#<ref> syntax was recorded, with no secrets, unrelated config, or config writes.
No commit was created by the implementer; final orchestrator/reviewer must inspect git status, diff, and recent log before any commit.
User decision: model selection should notify by default via toast, and debug_model_routing is not needed.
Follow-up source evidence is OpenCode SDK client.tui.showToast() plus opencode-acp reference behavior, not chat.message or session.prompt messaging.

Implementation summary for the follow-up contract is default-enabled toast notification with a minimal compatibility wrapper and in-session memory deduplication; no chat.message or session.prompt path is used.
Follow-up validation passed npm test (42 passed), npm run typecheck, npm run compile, npm run lint, npm run validate:no-model-ids, and npm run validate:dist.

patterns.name: Red-Green-Refactor by task
patterns.description: Implementation tasks after scaffold should write behavior/contract tests before production code.

patterns.name: Validation-as-contract
patterns.description: Sync, no-model-id, and optional dist validators are executable gates, not documentation-only checks.

patterns.name: PoC status labels
patterns.description: Use Verified/Pending/Blocked for install/load/dist/package-manager capability documentation.

patterns.name: Pure resolver before hook mutation
patterns.description: T03 exposes deterministic routing functions that later hooks can call, while actual chat/request model mutation remains deferred to T08 and Pending/Partial until proven.

patterns.name: Read-only upstream sync with audit manifest
patterns.description: T04 stores raw upstream agent bodies separately from schemaVersion 1 manifest metadata so validators can prove hash parity, canonical slug coverage, and orchestrator self-route exclusion.

patterns.name: Generated defaults before user override
patterns.description: Config injection builds generated agent defaults first, then merges existing OpenCode user config over those defaults so prompt, model, mode, permission, and unknown user fields are preserved.

patterns.name: Manifest as audit metadata
patterns.description: Runtime injection reads manifest routing/hash data but does not copy unsupported manifest metadata into OpenCode agent config.

patterns.name: Limited chat.params model override
patterns.description: T06 writes output.options.model only for plugin complexity_model resolutions; native agent.model and current-selected fallback paths intentionally avoid forced hook overrides.

patterns.name: Dist entrypoint validation as packaging guard
patterns.description: When package main/exports target dist/src, validate:dist must prove entrypoints, root declarations, import shape, and current forbidden assignment checks before install/release claims.

patterns.name: Deterministic dist forbidden-surface scan
patterns.description: validate:dist scans built dist/src line-by-line for forbidden capability/config strings and reports stable path:line reason failures, with defensive strings allowlisted only in known validation modules.

patterns.name: Default toast with session dedup
patterns.description: Model-selection notification should default to toast via a minimal compatibility wrapper and deduplicate in memory for the active plugin session so repeated routing decisions do not spam the UI.

gotchas: Same-wave dependencies are forbidden; keep one task per wave unless DAG validator proves independence.
conflicts_with entries must never target a task in the same wave.
Every new dependency edge must add a matching top-level contract.
Do not implement plugin code during planning-only tasks.
Do not replace upstream orchestration content with summarized semantics.
Do not claim chat.params can replace the actual request model until a no-provider runtime/mock proves a supported model override path.
Do not publish a final GitHub install command until the observed OpenCode GitHub source syntax is verified end-to-end for this repository.
T03 resolver model values are opaque strings; tests should keep using non-provider-shaped strings such as model-a or tier-model.
Do not add role_models, per-agent model maps, agent_complexity_models, provider router, or fallback chains in later routing work.
Preserve agents/generated/*.agent.md bodies across later waves; do not regenerate or normalize them unless sync validators and parity evidence remain green.
Keep gem-orchestrator excluded from child routing targets when generating config injection data.

GitHub commit metadata can be unknown-public-github-commit-unavailable if the unauthenticated public commits API is unavailable, but that weakens reproducibility and must stay visible in review notes.
Do not add model fields to newly injected generated agents; preserve existing user-provided model fields only.
Do not copy manifest audit metadata into OpenCode agent config; unsupported metadata belongs in agents/generated/manifest.json only.
Keep T03 priority chain intact in any later model-routing hook work: native agent.model before complexity_models[tier] before selected model.
@opencode-ai/plugin@1.15.13 chat.params output exposes output.options but no direct output model field; keep AC-20 Partial/Pending until runtime request mutation evidence exists.
When previewModelRouting upgrades tiers from reasoning-critical or risk signals, keep the tier space to simple, medium, and complex only and do not add per-agent/role model mappings.
validate:dist now scans dist/src for broad forbidden capability strings, but defensive validation/rejection code is intentionally allowlisted only in scoped validation modules.
Package exports pointing to dist/src create a freshness risk if dist is committed or regenerated outside validate:dist; rerun build and validate:dist and inspect git diff before any commit.

Final hardening left GitHub source install syntax Pending; follow-up redacted global config evidence moved syntax to format observed while GitHub package-manager/lifecycle behavior, remote GitHub dist necessity, repo end-to-end install/load, and AC-20 runtime request model mutation remain honest Pending or Partial/Pending risks.
A session.prompt marked ignored: true cannot be described as a true non-context toast equivalent; document that limitation explicitly.
Default notifications need in-session deduplication to avoid toast spam when the same routing decision repeats.
If client.tui.showToast is unavailable, compatibility behavior must silently skip notification rather than backfill with chat.message or session.prompt.

failure_modes.scenario: PoC cannot verify GitHub load locally.
failure_modes.mitigation: Keep docs in Pending/Blocked state and do not run optional dist/release claim path.

failure_modes.scenario: Sync conversion changes upstream body content.
failure_modes.mitigation: Preserve body verbatim or add explicit allowlist reason and evidence only for required compatibility metadata/header changes.

failure_modes.scenario: Model-routing hook cannot prove actual mutation without provider call.
failure_modes.mitigation: Mark routing capability limited/experimental and do not claim AC-20 as verified.

failure_modes.scenario: Security review finds forbidden capability or secret exposure.
failure_modes.mitigation: Remove feature/string, rerun validators, repeat T11 before T12.

failure_modes.scenario: Later hook work bypasses the T03 resolver priority chain.
symptoms: complexity model wins over native agent.model
selected model is ignored when tier model is missing
failure_modes.mitigation: Use resolveModel as the single pure routing contract and keep tests proving native_agent_model priority.

failure_modes.scenario: Unauthenticated public GitHub commit lookup is unavailable during sync.
failure_modes.mitigation: Keep the fallback explicit in manifest/review evidence, rerun sync when public commit metadata is available, and never use tokens or env secrets to force resolution.

failure_modes.scenario: Package exports target dist before GitHub install/dist behavior is proven.
symptoms: GitHub install/load path cannot resolve package entry
failure_modes.mitigation: Keep GitHub install, package-manager behavior, and dist necessity Pending until verified; only produce or validate dist if the PoC proves it necessary.

failure_modes.scenario: validate:dist allowlist grows beyond defensive validation modules.
symptoms: forbidden strings pass in capability implementation modules
dist scanner no longer reports deterministic path:line reason failures for forbidden config names
failure_modes.mitigation: Keep allowlisting limited to dist/src/routing/resolve-model.js(.d.ts) and dist/src/sync/validation.js(.d.ts); add failing validator tests before changing allowlist scope.

failure_modes.scenario: Plugin instance is rebuilt during the same user workflow.
symptoms: the same default model-selection toast can appear again after reload/reconstruction
failure_modes.mitigation: Document the behavior as low-risk follow-up compatibility debt; keep dedup scoped to session memory unless a stronger persistence requirement is explicitly approved.

decisions.decision: Use 12 waves with one task per wave.
rationale: Eliminates same-wave dependency edges
Improves reviewability and gate ordering

decisions.decision: Make T01 early feasibility PoC mandatory before scaffold-dependent implementation claims.
rationale: OpenCode plugin API was not confirmed at initial planning time; GitHub source syntax is now format-observed only
Docs/dist/release path must not overclaim

decisions.decision: Keep dist optional and PoC-dependent.
rationale: Reviewer/critic rejected default dist submission
GitHub install evidence must determine necessity

decisions.decision: Forbid real provider calls in default PoC and tests.
rationale: Avoid cost/side effects
Avoid exposing API keys/tokens
Plan can use dry-run/mock/local OpenCode load evidence

decisions.decision: Use PluginModule default export as the primary scaffold shape after T01.
rationale: Installed SDK exposes PluginModule
Fixture typecheck passed
Shape matches PRD section 5.7

decisions.decision: Keep GitHub install/load and dist necessity Pending after syntax-only follow-up evidence.
rationale: Official docs found in T01 document local and npm plugin paths
Follow-up evidence observed only redacted github:<owner>/<repo>#<ref> config syntax
Package export behavior depends on unverified GitHub install mechanics

decisions.decision: Use node:test plus built TypeScript output for the scaffold test gate.
rationale: Meets minimal dependency goal
Validates exported plugin shape without provider calls
Avoids introducing an extra test runner before feature work

decisions.decision: Keep sync and validation scripts as placeholders until later waves.
rationale: T02 scope is scaffold only
Later waves own sync and generated-asset behavior
Keeps the initial package buildable and typecheckable

decisions.decision: Missing or unknown complexity tiers fallback to medium in T03 pure routing.
rationale: Medium is the safest neutral tier across simple/complex uncertainty
Fallback is deterministic and reason-tagged for tests and later hook metadata

decisions.decision: Keep T03 model values opaque strings and avoid model ID validation inside resolveModel.
rationale: PRD forbids hardcoded concrete model IDs but user-provided model values are OpenCode-owned opaque strings
Security validation belongs at repository artifact scanning boundaries

decisions.decision: Reasoning-critical roles affect tier classification only, not model selection dimensions.
rationale: PRD AC-11/AC-12 forbid role_models and per-agent overrides
This preserves three-tier global routing while allowing safe tier hints

decisions.decision: Store synchronized upstream bodies in agents/generated and manifest metadata in agents/generated/manifest.json for T04.
rationale: Keeps generated artifacts stable and inspectable
Later T07 config injection can import or read one stable manifest path
Avoids rewriting upstream behavior while still recording audit metadata

decisions.decision: Use unauthenticated GitHub commits API only for best-effort commit metadata and raw.githubusercontent.com GET requests for agent bodies.
rationale: Satisfies read-only/no-token sync guard
Allows validation to proceed with explicit unknown value if public commit lookup is unavailable
Avoids reading or printing environment secrets

decisions.decision: Treat Wave 4/T04 as complete and continue to Wave 5.
rationale: Generated 16 upstream agent bodies and schemaVersion 1 manifest
Required sync/typecheck/build/test/model-id validations passed
Reviewer found no blocking issues

decisions.decision: Use a runtime filesystem loader instead of importing JSON/Markdown directly.
rationale: Current tsconfig does not include JSON or Markdown module imports
Filesystem loading works from source and dist paths
Hash parity checks keep generated prompt bodies preserved

decisions.decision: For existing OpenCode agent config, user fields override generated defaults.
rationale: Preserves user model, prompt, permission, mode, and unknown fields
Avoids destructive prompt overwrite while still filling missing generated fields

decisions.decision: Complete config hook injection in Wave 5/T05 while leaving Wave 6 and later validation/routing/docs gates pending.
rationale: Runtime loader and injection were implemented together using the generated manifest and bodies
Plan naming drift is recorded without inventing new tasks
GitHub install, dist/package-manager behavior, and actual request model mutation remain pending

decisions.decision: Keep manifest metadata separate from OpenCode agent config.
rationale: OpenCode agent config should receive only supported fields
Manifest remains the audit source for hashes, routingTargets, source URLs, commits, and sync metadata

decisions.decision: Treat Wave 6/T06 as complete and continue to Wave 7 while keeping AC-20 partial.
rationale: previewModelRouting and chat.params mock/type evidence passed all local guards
Reviewer found no blocking issues
Actual OpenCode request model mutation remains unverified without provider/API calls

decisions.decision: Expose package main and exports[./server] to dist/src/index.js and gate it with validate:dist.
rationale: OpenCode package target inspection expects exports[./server] or package main for server plugins
The first local package-path install failed without a server target
The second local package-path install passed after adding server entrypoints and built dist

decisions.decision: Document GitHub source install syntax as format observed / end-to-end Pending and provide only local/path development install guidance.
rationale: Official OpenCode plugin docs verified local and npm paths but not a final GitHub source syntax
Follow-up read-only global config inspection observed only a redacted plugin array format
No external publication or global config writes were used
Avoids overclaiming PRD AC-16

decisions.decision: Treat Wave 7/T07 as complete and continue to the final waves with pending remote-source risks preserved.
rationale: Local package-path install and 16-agent visibility were verified in a temp fixture
Reviewer found no blocking issues
GitHub source syntax format is observed only; GitHub package-manager behavior, remote dist necessity, AC-20 runtime mutation, and package exports-to-dist risk remain explicitly pending

decisions.decision: Treat final validation hardening as complete and ready for final review, not commit-ready without reviewer/orchestrator git inspection.
rationale: Full validation chain passed after broad dist forbidden-surface scanning was added
Pending GitHub/runtime risks remain accurately labeled
All implementation artifacts remain uncommitted and require final status/diff/log review before any commit

decisions.decision: Default model-selection notification should use toast and be enabled by default without debug_model_routing.
rationale: User explicitly chose default-visible notification behavior
OpenCode SDK exposes client.tui.showToast() and opencode-acp provides the reference direction
chat.message and session.prompt are intentionally excluded to avoid claiming a non-context notification path they do not faithfully provide

decisions.decision: Fallback behavior when toast is unavailable is silent skip only.
rationale: Maintains low-risk compatibility across SDK surfaces
Avoids misrepresenting ignored session prompts or chat messages as equivalent to a real toast
Keeps the wrapper minimal and non-invasive

conventions: Use docs/PRD.md as product source of truth.
Artifacts and IDs use concise English identifiers.
Package code should be TypeScript ESM unless T01 proves a different plugin shape is required.
Validators fail non-zero with actionable messages.
Documentation separates verified facts from pending or blocked PoC items.
Use docs/PRD.md, not docs/PRD.yaml, as the PRD source in this repository.
Manifest entries use schemaVersion 1 fields slug, localSlug, name, sourceUrl, rawUrl, upstreamCommit, sourceBodySha256, bodyPath, bodyBytes, syncBatchId, syncedAt, and metadata.
Config hook injection must be non-destructive: generated defaults first, existing user config wins.
Newly injected generated agents omit model; only pre-existing OpenCode user config may carry model.
Model-routing documentation and review notes must label chat.params request mutation as Partial/Pending unless runtime evidence proves AC-20 without provider/API calls.
Before any commit, inspect git status, git diff, and git log --oneline -10; if dist is committed, ensure clean regeneration and validate:dist green.
validate:dist allowlists defensive validation/rejection strings only in dist/src/routing/resolve-model.js(.d.ts) and dist/src/sync/validation.js(.d.ts).

Default model-selection notification documentation must describe toast-first behavior, minimal compatibility wrapping, in-session memory deduplication, and silent skip when client.tui.showToast is unavailable.

guards_verified: generated bodies are preserved upstream bodies
no forbidden model dimensions/provider router/standalone auto/slash/telemetry/global config
review_warnings: docs/PRD.yaml absent; docs/PRD.md is the actual PRD source.
Commit API fallback unknown-public-github-commit-unavailable can reduce sync reproducibility if public metadata is unavailable.
next_wave_guards: preserve generated bodies
exclude gem-orchestrator from child routing
do not introduce forbidden model dimensions/provider router/standalone auto/slash/telemetry/global config
keep model values opaque
do not read or print env secrets
keep GitHub install, dist, and actual request model mutation pending until proven
run validators
add parity checks for conversion layer if body diffs appear

install_poc_findings.local_load: Verified local package-path plugin install and 16-agent visibility in temp fixture only.
install_poc_findings.github_source_syntax: format_observed_end_to_end_pending; user-approved redacted global config inspection observed github:<owner>/<repo>#<ref>, but this repo was not verified end-to-end from GitHub.
install_poc_findings.package_manager_behavior: Partial; npm plugins are documented as Bun-installed; path specs are file plugins; GitHub spec behavior remains unverified.
install_poc_findings.dist_need: Partial/prepared; package entrypoints targeting dist require built dist/src files.

t07_install_load_results.dist_strategy_decision: Prepare dist/src inclusion and validate:dist because package-style OpenCode plugin detection/imports require built entrypoints when exports target dist; keep remote GitHub install Pending until verified.
guards_verified: no env secret reads or output
commands_passed: opencode plugin local path in temp fixture
opencode agent list local fixture showed 16 gem-* agents
review_warnings: validate:dist currently checks package entrypoints, root declaration/import shape, API-key-like assignments, and provider-style model assignments, but a broader forbidden string scan is still recommended for deterministic enforcement.
Keep GitHub source syntax as format observed / end-to-end Pending unless verified end-to-end with safe remote-source evidence.
Keep package-manager behavior Partial/Pending for GitHub specs.
Keep AC-20 Partial/Pending until actual OpenCode runtime request model mutation is proven without provider/API calls.
next_wave_guards: no provider model IDs, provider examples, API keys, secrets, telemetry, slash commands, provider routers, virtual models, fallback chains, per-agent overrides, role_models, or agent_complexity_models
no global OpenCode config writes
if dist is committed, regenerate clean and keep validate:dist green
inspect git status, diff, and recent log before any commit
pending_items_preserved: GitHub source install syntax format observed; end-to-end install/load remains Pending
remote GitHub dist necessity remains Pending
actual OpenCode request model mutation AC-20 remains Partial/Pending
package exports to dist/src risk remains pending until remote source behavior is verified

guards_verified: generated prompts are read from agents/generated/*.agent.md without body rewrites
existing user agent fields are preserved
newly injected agents do not receive model fields
no unsupported metadata is injected into OpenCode agent config
review_warnings: docs/PRD.yaml is absent; docs/PRD.md is the actual PRD source.
Plan labels drifted because config hook injection was implemented in Wave 5/T05 while T07 still names config injection validation/load evidence.
package exports point to dist but dist is not present or committed; install behavior remains Pending.
next_wave_guards: preserve agents/generated/*.agent.md bodies
do not add model fields to newly injected agents
preserve existing user fields including model, prompt, mode, permission, and unknown fields
keep gem-orchestrator excluded from self-routing
do not inject unsupported metadata into OpenCode agent config
do not read, print, or persist secrets or env values
do not introduce concrete provider model IDs or provider examples

source_evidence: User decision: default show model-selection notification
implementation_summary: Do not use chat.message or session.prompt as the notification transport.
Use a minimal compatibility wrapper around client.tui.showToast when available.
Deduplicate repeated notifications in session-memory scope only.
compatibility_constraints: session.prompt with ignored: true is not equivalent to a true non-context toast and must not be documented that way.
If client.tui.showToast is unavailable, silently skip the notification rather than substituting chat.message or session.prompt.
Default-visible notifications must control repeat prompting to avoid toast spam.
model_selection_toast_followup.follow_up_risk: Plugin instance reconstruction resets in-memory dedup state, so the same toast may be shown again within a broader user workflow.

validation_hardening_summary: validate:dist scans dist/src line-by-line and reports deterministic path:line reason failures.

Forbidden dist strings now include telemetry, slash commands, provider routers/routing, virtual models, fallback chains, standalone auto model, role_models, agent_complexity_models, per-agent model override dimensions, global OpenCode config writes, API-key/token/secret-like assignments, and provider-style model assignments.
Allowlist is limited to defensive validation strings in dist/src/routing/resolve-model.js(.d.ts) and dist/src/sync/validation.js(.d.ts).

defensive validation strings scoped to known validation/rejection modules
